denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This letter concerns the prior authorization request your provider submitted to continue adjuvant pembrolizumab and complete a planned course of therapy following melanoma surgery. After review under the applicable Cigna Medical Coverage Policy, the request is not authorized. Determination date: May 25, 2026.

Continuation of this therapy requires an active prior authorization on file. The prior authorization previously approved for this treatment has lapsed, so a new authorization is required before coverage can continue. In evaluating this continuation request, we applied the Cigna Medical Coverage Policy for immunotherapy in melanoma (2020), and on the records reviewed the criteria for continued authorization are not met.

You or your provider may request, at no charge, a copy of the specific coverage policy and the documents relied upon in reaching this decision. If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, a peer-to-peer review with a Cigna medical director may be arranged by calling the number on your member ID card.

You have the right to file an internal appeal of this decision. To file an internal appeal, you or your authorized representative must submit a written request within 180 days of the date of this letter, along with any additional clinical information you would like considered. Appeals may be sent to the address shown on your Explanation of Benefits.

Sincerely,
Cigna Coverage Review Department
clinical_context: The patient is a 52-year-old man with resected stage IIIB cutaneous melanoma (BRAF wild-type, a 2.1 mm primary with one positive sentinel node, wide local excision completed) who began adjuvant pembrolizumab to reduce the risk of recurrence. He is approximately seven months into the planned twelve-month adjuvant course, has tolerated therapy well aside from grade 1 hypothyroidism managed with levothyroxine, and has no evidence of recurrence on surveillance imaging. The prior authorization covering this treatment lapsed during active therapy after his oncology practice submitted a renewal that was not processed before the existing authorization expired, an administrative gap rather than a change in clinical status. His oncologist documents that interrupting adjuvant immunotherapy before completion of the planned one-year course is expected to reduce its recurrence-prevention benefit, and that current NCCN guidance supports completing the full adjuvant pembrolizumab course - superseding the 2020 coverage policy the insurer applied. The determination letter provided notice of internal appeal only and gave no notice of the member's right to external independent review, a procedural protection required for an adverse benefit determination.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: matrix_cell matches field-for-field (Cigna, Prior Authorization, oncology, 41-55, M, continuation_of_care_lapsed). Melanoma is oncology; adjuvant pembrolizumab continuation realistic. Rationale precisely enacts continuation_of_care_lapsed (lapsed re-auth). Age 52 in band; not 71+, null employer_archetype fine.
evidence_quotes: "sub_tactic": "continuation_of_care_lapsed"
the prior authorization for the ongoing therapy has lapsed and a new authorization is required before treatment can continue

scenario_realism.reasoning: Frustratingly realistic. Adjuvant pembrolizumab over a one-year course for resected stage IIIB melanoma is standard of care; a lapsed re-auth interrupting active oncology treatment is textbook. Stale 2020 policy superseded by NCCN and an omitted external-review notice are realistic exploitable gaps. No anachronism or mismatch.
evidence_quotes: the prior authorization for the ongoing therapy has lapsed and a new authorization is required before treatment can continue
The 2020 policy the insurer applied is superseded by current NCCN guidance supporting completion of adjuvant pembrolizumab

insurer_voice.reasoning: Brands as Cigna and anchors to 'the applicable Cigna Medical Coverage Policy' with no wrong-insurer tells (no InterQual/Optum), so above a 1. But it never invokes Cigna's signature criteria (MCG / 'Medical Necessity Guidelines') or step-therapy framing; reads as generic payer denial cadence. Anchor 3.
evidence_quotes: After review under the applicable Cigna Medical Coverage Policy, the request is not authorized.
a peer-to-peer review with a Cigna medical director may be arranged by calling the number on your member ID card
insurer_voice.improvement: Cite Cigna's named criteria (Medical Necessity Guidelines / MCG) to read unmistakably as Cigna.

denial_logic.reasoning: Fits continuation_of_care_lapsed: denies continuation because the prior auth lapsed. Cites a plausible vaguely-named 2020 policy applied circularly ('criteria for continued authorization are not met' without naming a threshold). Procedural rather than clinical; wide appeal room. No hallucinated guideline.
evidence_quotes: The prior authorization previously approved for this treatment has lapsed, so a new authorization is required before coverage can continue.
we applied the Cigna Medical Coverage Policy for immunotherapy in melanoma (2020), and on the records reviewed the criteria for continued authorization are not met

clinical_realism.reasoning: Adjuvant pembrolizumab for resected stage IIIB melanoma is guideline-concordant (KEYNOTE-054/-716 era); staging coherent (2.1 mm primary, one positive sentinel node, BRAF wild-type steers to immunotherapy); 7-of-12 months is realistic; grade 1 hypothyroidism on levothyroxine is the most common dose-appropriate irAE. No invented drugs or impossible courses.
evidence_quotes: resected stage IIIB cutaneous melanoma (BRAF wild-type, a 2.1 mm primary with one positive sentinel node, wide local excision completed) who began adjuvant pembrolizumab
has tolerated therapy well aside from grade 1 hypothyroidism managed with levothyroxine

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: Pembrolizumab is FDA-approved and NCCN-endorsed adjuvant therapy for resected stage IIB-IV melanoma (KEYNOTE-054), with a one-year standard course. Exact category match for the resected/adjuvant setting.
evidence_quotes: Resected stage IIIB cutaneous melanoma (C43.9), on adjuvant therapy
Continuation of adjuvant pembrolizumab to complete the planned one-year course

Administrative-gap frustration is not incitement. Cigna commercial, in scope. No banned topic.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Two strong vectors: no external-review notice (required procedural protection), and reliance on the outdated 2020 policy where current NCCN supersedes it to support completing the course. Patient clearly meets standard of care (7 months into a 12-month course, well tolerated, no recurrence); the lapse was an administrative gap. Only defense is the technical lapsed authorization.
exploitable_weaknesses: No notice of external independent review right, a required procedural protection for an adverse benefit determination
Denial applies the outdated 2020 Cigna coverage policy while current NCCN guidance superseding it supports completing the full adjuvant course
strong_defenses: An active prior authorization had genuinely lapsed, so a new authorization is technically required before coverage can continue

current NCCN guidance supports completing the full adjuvant pembrolizumab course - superseding the 2020 coverage policy the insurer applied
